title: The origin of humans and early human societies
description: Practice your skills with a quiz on prehistory!
topic_path: The origin of humans and early human societies
The origin of humans and early human societies

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 2]]

**This stone tool is most representative of tools from which of the following periods?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Hand axe found in Winchester, England.
options.caption: Hand axe found in Winchester, England. Image credit: [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hand_axe#/media/File:Flint_hand_axe.JPG).

choices.content: Neolithic
Paleolithic
Bronze Age
Agricultural Revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**An archaeologist could best use these 12,000-year-old stone tools as evidence of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Tools found in Bernifal cave in Meyrals, Dordogne, France, estimated to be 12,000 - 10,000 years old.
options.caption: Tools found in Bernifal cave in Meyrals, Dordogne, France, estimated to be 12,000 - 10,000 years old. Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Burins_and_blades_-_Bernifal_-_Meyrals_-_MNP.jpg).

choices.content: The prevalence of stone in caves
A move away from reliance on natural resources
Increasingly sophisticated stone technology
The transition to bronze and iron tools

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage before answering the question.

> "Knowing how to use fire may have helped the early humans who left Africa 50,000 to 60,000 years ago to cope with colder conditions in Europe. It may also have given them a big advantage over the resident Neanderthals they encountered. By 35,000 years ago the Neanderthals, a sub-species of human whose own origins were in Africa, were mostly extinct."

From _The Telegraph_, Article on Stone Age humans (2009).

**Which of the following claims about human migration into Europe would this passage provide evidence for?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Fire was a tool that helped modern humans to displace Neanderthals in Europe
Hominids did not live in Europe prior to modern humans migrating there
Early humans initially invented fire as a weapon against Neanderthals
Modern humans were only able to migrate to Europe because they mastered fire

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the map and your knowledge of world history, which of the following best describes how Paleolithic humans migrated between continents?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Map of the spread of _Homo sapiens_ (likely routes of migration in red).
options.caption: Spread of _Homo sapiens_ (likely routes of migration in red.) Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Spreading_homo_sapiens.svg).

choices.content: In large groups of people in order to establish agricultural villages
In small groups of people that could move quickly and easily
In small groups of people in order to establish agricultural villages
In large groups of people that could move quickly and easily

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below before answering the question.

> "That cave is now called Jebel Irhoud, and bones of its former occupants have been recently unearthed by an international team of scientists. They mark the earliest fossilized remains of _Homo sapiens_ ever found. Until now, that honor belonged to two Ethiopian fossils that are 160,000 and 195,000 years old respectively. But the Jebel Irhoud bones, and the stone tools that were uncovered with them, are far older—around 315,000 years old, with a possible range of 280,000 to 350,000 years."

From Ed Yong, "Scientists Have Found the Oldest Known Human Fossils." _The Atlantic_ (2017).

**How did the discovery of human fossils and tools at Jebel Irhoud affect existing theories of human migration?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It potentially placed the first _Homo sapiens_ in a different location, and much earlier in time, than previously thought
It provided additional support for the theory that _Homo sapiens_ first appeared in Ethiopia
It provided evidence that _Homo sapiens_ migrated to find stones that they could make into  tools
It provided evidence that _Homo sapiens_ didn't migrate, but rather evolved independently in two locations

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage before answering the question.

> "Knowing how to use fire may have helped the early humans who left Africa 50,000 to 60,000 years ago to cope with colder conditions in Europe. It may also have given them a big advantage over the resident Neanderthals they encountered. By 35,000 years ago the Neanderthals, a sub-species of human whose own origins were in Africa, were mostly extinct."

From _The Telegraph_, Article on Stone Age humans (2009).

**Which of the following claims about hominids would this passage provide evidence for?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Early human groups exchanged information and technology with Neanderthals
Early human groups exchanged information and technology with one another
Neanderthal groups exchanged information and technology with one another
Neanderthal groups taught early humans new ideas and technologies
